title: CLI alignment: unified UX across ecosystem
description: Align all four CLIs (mulch, sd, cn, overstory) on framework, output, error handling, and command patterns. Mulch is the reference implementation. Big bang for Seeds and Canopy; incremental for Overstory.

title: Seeds: adopt Commander for CLI parsing
description: Seeds currently uses manual process.argv parsing with a switch statement in src/index.ts. Migrate to Commander (like Mulch) to get: structured arg/option definitions, auto-generated help with argument types and choices, .addHelpText() for detailed per-command docs, built-in version display. This is a big-bang rewrite of the CLI layer — command logic stays, only the parsing/routing changes. Reference: mulch/src/cli.ts for the pattern. Each command in src/commands/ should export a register function that takes a Commander program instance.

title: Overstory multi-repo mode: spawn agents into sub-repos
description: Add --repo flag to overstory sling/merge so the root overstory instance can spawn agents into sub-repo worktrees (seeds, mulch, canopy, overstory) and merge them back. Config adds a repos map in .overstory/config.yaml. Mail/sessions/metrics stay in root .overstory/ for unified coordination. Main changes: sling.ts (worktree creation targeting sub-repo git dir), merge.ts (repo-aware merge), config.ts (repos map).

title: Seeds: adopt chalk for output formatting
description: Seeds uses raw ANSI escape codes in src/output.ts with manual TTY/NO_COLOR detection. Migrate to chalk (like Mulch) for: consistent color API across the ecosystem, automatic terminal capability detection, cleaner output code. Current Seeds patterns to migrate: c.green/c.red/c.bold/c.dim helper functions → chalk.green/chalk.red/chalk.bold/chalk.dim. Status icons (●/◐/○) and printIssueOneLine/printIssueFull formatting should be preserved but use chalk underneath. The outputJson/printSuccess/printError helpers should match Mulch's patterns (✔ green for success, Error: red for errors).

title: Seeds: align error handling with ecosystem pattern
description: Seeds uses process.exit(1) in its main catch block. Migrate to process.exitCode = 1 (Mulch pattern) for better testability — allows event loop to drain and vitest/bun test to capture output cleanly. Also standardize JSON error shape: currently { success: false, command, error } which already matches Mulch. Ensure all commands use consistent error surfacing: console.error() with chalk.red in human mode, structured JSON in --json mode.

title: Canopy: adopt Commander for CLI parsing
description: Canopy uses manual process.argv parsing with a switch statement in src/index.ts, plus per-command flag parsing via local helpers. Migrate to Commander (like Mulch) to get: structured arg/option definitions, auto-generated help, choice validation, .addHelpText() for detailed docs. Big-bang rewrite of CLI layer. Each command in src/commands/ should export a register function. Reference: mulch/src/cli.ts. Notable: Canopy has subcommands (schema create/show/list, schema rule add) — Commander supports this via .command().command() nesting.

title: Canopy: adopt chalk for output formatting
description: Canopy uses raw ANSI codes in src/output.ts with manual NO_COLOR/TTY detection. Migrate to chalk (like Mulch) for consistent ecosystem UX. Current Canopy patterns to migrate: c.bold/c.dim/c.green/c.red/c.yellow/c.cyan/c.blue helpers → chalk equivalents. Preserve the humanOut/jsonOut/errorOut helper pattern but use chalk underneath. Status indicators (✓/✗/→) should match Mulch's style (✔ for success, Error: prefix for failures).

title: Canopy: align error handling with ecosystem pattern
description: Canopy has a custom ExitError class and uses both process.exit(1) and throw ExitError(1). Migrate to process.exitCode = 1 pattern (Mulch standard) for testability. The ExitError class can remain for internal flow control but the top-level handler should set process.exitCode rather than calling process.exit(). JSON error shape should be standardized: { success: false, command, error }.

title: Overstory: incrementally adopt Commander for CLI parsing
description: Overstory uses manual argv parsing with local getFlag()/hasFlag()/parseArgs() helpers across 30+ commands. Incremental migration plan: (1) Add Commander as dependency, (2) Migrate simple commands first (init, status, clean, doctor), (3) Then migrate complex commands with subcommands (agents, coordinator, supervisor, mail, run, group, spec, hooks, worktree), (4) Finally migrate observability commands (dashboard, inspect, trace, errors, replay, feed, logs). Each migration should be a standalone PR. Reference: mulch/src/cli.ts for the command registration pattern.

title: Overstory: adopt chalk for output formatting
description: Overstory has its own ANSI color system in src/logging/color.ts with manual escape codes and NO_COLOR/FORCE_COLOR/TERM=dumb detection. Incrementally migrate to chalk: (1) Replace src/logging/color.ts internals with chalk while keeping the same API surface, (2) Then gradually migrate direct color usage in commands to chalk calls. This can be done in parallel with Commander migration. Overstory's color system is more sophisticated (has FORCE_COLOR) — chalk handles all of this natively.

title: Overstory: align error handling with ecosystem pattern
description: Overstory has a rich error hierarchy (OverstoryError → ConfigError, AgentError, HierarchyError, MailError, ValidationError, MergeError, WorktreeError) with error codes. This is the most mature error system in the ecosystem. Keep the error class hierarchy but align exit behavior: use process.exitCode = 1 instead of process.exit(1) where possible. JSON error shape should include the error code field: { success: false, command, error, code }. The error code pattern could be adopted by other tools eventually.

title: Standardize shared command patterns across ecosystem
description:
All four tools have overlapping commands (sync, prime, doctor, onboard) with inconsistent flags and behavior. Align them:

**sync**: All tools have sync. Standardize flags: --status (dry-run check) across all. Currently consistent.

**prime**: All tools have prime. Mulch has --verbose/--full/--format/--files/--context. Seeds has --compact. Canopy has --compact/--export. Overstory has its own patterns. Align: all should support --compact and --format at minimum.

**doctor**: Mulch has --fix. Seeds has --fix. Overstory has --category/--verbose. Canopy lacks doctor. Align: all should support --fix and --verbose at minimum.

**onboard**: Mulch and Seeds and Canopy all have onboard. Standardize the --check/--stdout flags across all.

**help text**: All --help output should follow Commander's format: Usage line, Description, Arguments, Options with types/defaults, Examples via addHelpText.

**--json**: All tools support --json. Ensure consistent behavior: always outputs to stdout, errors still to stderr, response shape { success, command, data/error }.

title: Add shell completion support to Seeds and Canopy
description: Overstory has shell completions (--completions bash/zsh/fish) via src/commands/completions.ts. Mulch has no completions (Commander can generate basic ones). Seeds and Canopy have none. Once Commander is adopted across all tools, add shell completion generation. Commander has built-in completion support or can be extended. Overstory's completion system (CommandDef/FlagDef/SubcommandDef types) is a good reference but should be replaced with Commander's native approach once migrated.

title: Standardize version management across ecosystem
description:
Version handling differs across tools:
- Mulch (0.5.0): VERSION in package.json, program.version() in cli.ts, has version check utility (npm registry)
- Seeds (0.2.0): VERSION const in src/index.ts + package.json, manual --version flag
- Canopy (0.1.2): VERSION const in src/index.ts + package.json, manual --version flag  
- Overstory (0.6.0): VERSION const in src/index.ts + package.json, manual --version flag

All have scripts/version-bump.ts. Once Commander is adopted, use program.version() consistently. Consider whether each tool should have an update-check like Mulch's getLatestVersion().

title: Stop hook error: mulch not found in agent PATH
closeReason: Fixed manually. Follow-up doctor checks tracked in overstory seeds-93a8.

title: Add --root flag to sd create for writing to project root .seeds/
description: When agents run in git worktrees, sd create writes to the worktree .seeds/ directory. If that branch is never merged (e.g. lead agents), those issues are lost. Add a --root flag that writes directly to the project root .seeds/ instead of the worktree copy, so issues survive worktree cleanup regardless of merge status.

title: Sapling × Overstory: Phase 1 runtime adapter
description: Cross-repo tracking issue. Implement sapling as an overstory runtime adapter with direct subprocess management (no tmux). See docs/sapling-overstory-integration.md Phase 1. Work split between overstory/ and sapling/.
closeReason: Phase 1 runtime adapter complete across both repos. 11 issues closed.

title: Sapling × Overstory: Phase 2 native hook system
description: Cross-repo tracking issue. Sapling-native hooks/guards configured by overstory, enforced inside sapling tool registry. Per-tool-call NDJSON events. See docs/sapling-overstory-integration.md Phase 2.

title: Sapling × Overstory: Phase 3 native ecosystem integration
description: Cross-repo tracking issue. Direct bun:sqlite access to mail.db/metrics.db, direct mulch/seeds calls from sapling agents. Eliminates subprocess overhead. See docs/sapling-overstory-integration.md Phase 3.

title: Test sapling runtime with MiniMax backend
Diagnose and fix sapling-overstory integration for MiniMax backend
description:
## Context

Attempted to run overstory with --runtime sapling using MiniMax as the LLM backend. Multiple issues surfaced that need systematic resolution.

## Known Issues

1. CLAUDECODE env leak: spawning sapling from Claude Code leaks CLAUDECODE=1, auto-selecting sdk backend without an API key
2. Missing prompt argument: buildDirectSpawn() was not passing a user prompt, causing sapling to hang on stdin
3. Gateway provider translation: overstory sets ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN (for CC runtime) but sapling SDK needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
4. Model alias resolution: overstory resolves gateway models to alias "sonnet" but sapling needs the actual model name
5. End-to-end flow untested

## IMPORTANT: Talk to the developer first

The agent working on this MUST have a conversation with the human developer before implementing anything. Ask about:
- What they expect sapling <> overstory integration to look like
- How MiniMax and other non-Anthropic providers should be configured
- Whether the gateway provider pattern is right for sapling or if sapling should handle provider config independently
- What sapling/.env is intended to do vs overstory config.yaml providers

Partial fixes exist in overstory/src/runtimes/sapling.ts (uncommitted) but were written without full understanding of the intended architecture. Review before committing.

## Files with uncommitted changes

- overstory/src/runtimes/sapling.ts (buildEnv and buildDirectSpawn changes)
- overstory/.overstory/config.yaml (added minimax provider config)

title: Sapling goes zombie immediately when spawned by overstory (headless)
E2E verification: sapling-overstory integration with MiniMax
Simplify overstory sapling runtime to passthrough buildEnv after sapling env var compat
Smoke test: sapling scout reads README
ov sling --runtime sapling: deployConfig called with undefined overlay skips guards.json
Smoke test: sapling scout reads README via overstory
Test: sapling runtime scout exploration of project structure
closeReason: Explored worktree structure: found standard os-eco files (README.md, CLAUDE.md, SAPLING.md), branding/, .overstory/, .claude/commands/, and tool directories (.seeds/, .mulch/, .canopy/). Test scout exploration verified successfully.
